ab: mo cihi ’o mimza tmahmʉhmʉsku zou pasuya ’o ongkosi, la luema’payo’ʉ mahafa ’to macucumasi.
ch: 我有一個同學叫pasuya，他常常忘記帶東西。
en: I have a classmate named pasuya, he often forgets to bring things.

ab: ho la tmopsʉ, la mi’unu tomo tmahmʉhmʉskʉ ho yuevaho to lesmapayo’ʉ ho letposi.
ch: 上課的時候，他跟同學借鉛筆、橡皮擦或是筆記本。
en: During class, he borrowed pencils, erasers or notebooks from his classmates.

ab: y’ʉnʉskʉ, i’popsohʉ, i’toteuhu, lahen’a yuvahneni to nacucuma.
ch: 一次、兩次、三次，大家會借東西給他。
en: Once, twice, three times, everyone would lend him something.

ab: ati’nghi pantolata kuici hioa, lata asngʉcʉ yuvaho to macucumahe ho oh’te yooveineni.
ch: 但是他有個壞習慣，經常借了東西卻不還。
en: But he has a bad habit of often borrowing things but not returning them.

ab: ko’ko o’a na ohe’la ucia poa yuvahneni to macucumahe.
ch: 最後大家都不想借他東西了。
en: In the end, no one wanted to borrow anything from him.

ch: 有一天，我們大家一起去郊遊。
en: One day, we all went on an outing together.

ab: momza seolʉano emo’usnumaica, akameosʉ na’no mici te’i ’o pasuya, at’enghi ’iamamita tapayo’ʉ mahafo tola titha mahisipcoo ci kami.
ch: 路途中，pasuya想上廁所，但他忘記帶衛生紙。
en: On the way, pasuya wanted to go to the bathroom, but he forgot to bring toilet paper.

ab: mita mi’usnu to mihini tmahmʉhmʉsku ’e pasuya ho yuvaho to letithamahisi teta akoyu no ohse’u, atng’ihi mo acʉhʉ mainca, maezo o’a mi’os’a mahafo.
ch: 他跟同學借衛生紙，可是大家都回他說，我也沒有帶耶。
en: He borrowed toilet paper from his classmates, but everyone told him that I didn’t bring them either.

ab: mitacu amio tmumongsi ’e pasuya. mita sʉ’no ho mainca, maincis’a o’te poa yuvahoa to letithamahisi.
ch: pasuya急的哭了出來。他生氣的說，為什麼大家都不借我衛生紙!
en: pasuya burst into tears. He said angrily, why don’t everyone lend me toilet paper!

ab: yainca to mihimn’i tmahmʉhmʉskʉ, ’a miko no’so ’ote iachi mahafo, a’vinano inante yuvahnenisu ci macucuma, ana tekos’a ahta yoveineni.
ch: 同學告訴他，因為你都不自己帶，而且借給你的東西，你都不會還!
en: The classmate told him, because you didn’t bring it yourself, and you wouldn’t return the things lent to you!

ab: micu hucma tec’u uhuto ’oyonatmopsʉ, mo sʉfeʉnʉ ’o pasuya uhto ’oyonatmpsʉ, ita acʉha yoveineni to mihimni tmahmʉhmʉskʉ ’o ohtala yuvaha ci macucuma.
ch: 隔天上學，pasuya很早就到學校了，pasuya把跟同學借的東西都還給同學們。
en: The next day at school, pasuya arrived at school early and returned everything he had borrowed from his classmates.

ab: mi’usnu to mihimn’i tmahmʉhmʉskʉ ho ’aveoveoyʉ ’e pasuya, mainca, ta’ucula iachi mahavo no macucuma, ’aveoveoneni to mimza tmahmʉhmʉskʉ ihingn’i yuvahneni to macucuma ne auyusi.
ch: pasuya向同學們道歉，並說他以後一定會自己帶東西，也謝謝同學們之前願意借他東西。
en: pasuya apologized to the students and said that he would definitely bring his own things in the future, and thanked the classmates for their willingness to lend him things before.
